findings.description: DEQ logo placement differs between PDF and HTML
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the DEQ logo is centered at the top of the page. In the HTML, the logo is left-aligned at the top of the page.

In the PDF, 'Inactive Hazardous Sites Branch' and 'Annual Report Form for Perpetual Land-Use Restrictions' are both bold and centered together as a heading block. In the HTML, 'Inactive Hazardous Sites Branch' is a larger standalone heading with a horizontal rule below it, and 'Annual Report Form for Perpetual Land-Use Restrictions' appears as a separate subheading below the rule.

In the PDF, the checklist items have YES/NA column headers with indented text items below. In the HTML, checkbox squares (☐) are placed inline before each item. The functional content is preserved but the visual presentation of the YES/NA selection mechanism differs.

findings.description: First checklist item missing second checkbox column
findings.evidence: In the PDF, each checklist item has both YES and NA options. In the HTML, the first item ('All restrictions in the recorded Declaration...') has only one checkbox, while items 2-5 have two checkboxes. Items 6 and 7 also have only one checkbox each.

In the PDF, 'YES' and 'NA' are underlined and bold as column headers with '(not applicable)' in italics. In the HTML, 'YES' and 'NA' are bold/underlined but '(not applicable)' is in regular italic, which is acceptable, but the column header nature is less clear.

findings.description: The HTML uses h1 for 'Inactive Hazardous Sites Branch' and h2 for 'Annual Report Form for Perpetual Land-Use Restrictions', but in the PDF these are both part of the same document title/header. Additionally, 'Property Owner Certification Statement' is rendered as h3 in the HTML, but it is a bold section heading in the PDF at the same structural level — not a sub-section of the checklist. The hierarchy is slightly misleading but not critically broken.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'Inactive Hazardous Sites Branch' and 'Annual Report Form for Perpetual Land-Use Restrictions' appear as a combined title. HTML: h1 and h2 respectively. 'Property Owner Certification Statement' is h3 in HTML but is a primary section heading in the PDF.

findings.description: The first checklist item is missing its YES/NA dual-checkbox structure. In the PDF, all seven items have two checkboxes (YES and NA columns). In the HTML, the first item only has one checkbox symbol (☐), while items 2-5 have two checkbox symbols. Items 6 and 7 also only have one checkbox symbol each. This inconsistency misrepresents the form structure.
findings.evidence: PDF shows two columns (YES and NA) for all seven checklist items. HTML first item: '☐ All restrictions in the recorded Declaration...' (one checkbox). Items 2-5: '☐ ☐' (two checkboxes). Items 6-7: '☐' (one checkbox each).

findings.description: The HTML contains a nested <main> element inside another <main> element, which is a structural duplication of the semantic container. The form content is wrapped in both an outer <main> and an inner <main>.
